calibration.repoShape: TypeScript server framework/build tool with runtime presets, CLI lazy commands, and rollup dynamic-require helpers
calibration.safetyPosture: Clone-only source scan; no target installs, package-manager scripts, target tests, target builds, submodules, or npx were run.
calibration.scopeQuestion: Can Axiom's opt-in dynamic warning family identify non-literal runtime loading in production source without turning literal dynamic imports into warnings?
calibration.commandSurface: axi infer --json + axi graph --json + axi diff + axi observe --baseline
calibration.mainSignal: Nitro source produced four focused dynamic dependency expressions for runtime/server entry loading and dynamic-require helper code, while literal dynamic imports stayed graph evidence rather than advisory warnings.
calibration.decision: Record dynamic dependency warnings as useful review evidence for runtime loading blind spots; do not change scanner, resolver, or gate behavior from this run.
calibration.codeChanged: No Axiom validator code changed; this is a no-install dynamic calibration run.
calibration.followUp: Compare with one more dynamic-heavy repo before making dynamic warnings more prominent in onboarding or release notes.

reviewStory.summary: Starter contract inferred 2 modules and 1 observed module edge from 158 source files.
reviewStory.setup: Scanned 158 source files and 738 imports; 8 candidate groups became 2 starter modules. This is a current-graph snapshot, not declared architecture intent yet.

pressures.title: Collapsed cycle: MixedCycle
pressures.description: 1 cycle was merged so the starter contract mirrors current code without immediately failing on declared dependency cycles. Review cycle-breaking candidates before treating the merged module as intended architecture.

pressures.title: Review inferred dependencies
pressures.description: 1 observed module edge became `depends on` lines with sample import evidence. Confirm each edge is intended before using this draft as a gate.

reviewStory.nextStep: Review collapsed-cycle candidates, rename or split merged modules if needed, then run `axi observe --root . --spec <draft.axi> --markdown`.
reviewStory.caveat: Inference reads static imports and folder/package shape. It can lower authoring cost, but humans still decide module names, visibility, layers, accepted debt, and which edges are real architecture intent.
